title: Golden Globes 2026: What Paul Mescal Told Chloé Zhao Before Hamnet's Best Picture Win
text: At the 2026 Golden Globes ceremony hosted by Nikki Glaser on January 11, the Shakespeare-inspired film Hamnet claimed the Best Picture Drama award, competing against titles including Sentimental Value, Frankenstein, and Sinners. Director Chloé Zhao accepted the prize and shared a meaningful comment from star Paul Mescal. She explained that Mescal had told her the film taught him about learning to be vulnerable enough to allow ourselves to be seen and embracing our imperfect aspects. Producer Steven Spielberg praised Zhao's vision, saying: I felt there was really only one filmmaker on the face of the planet who could tell this story. He called her the exceptional, exceptional, exceptional Chloé Zhao. The victory was particularly notable as Hamnet also earned Best Female Actor for Jessie Buckley, who portrayed Agnes Hathaway, William Shakespeare's wife in the family drama.
